web.markdown:
![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/l4xtK6OlOxE/maxresdefault.jpg)
# [How to Automatically Save Fathom Meeting Recordings to Google Drive](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=l4xtK6OlOxE)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Marco Serafini](https://www.youtube.com/@marco_ith)
**Uploaded at**: 2025-01-27
**Published at**: 
**Length**: 03:36
**Views**: 1790
**Likes**: 12
**Category**: Science & Technology

## Description

```
Learn how to back up your #Fathom meeting recordings to Google Drive using #Zapier! 🚀
In this quick tutorial, I’ll guide you through automating the process to keep your meetings organized.

💡 Get 1 month of Fathom Premium for free with my referral link: https://fathom.video/invite/DjU8rw

----------

🚀 Subscribe to my newsletter for free: https://www.mindstoneconsulting.net/newsletter

-- MY AFFILIATE LINKS --
✅ ClickUp: https://try.web.clickup.com/6h62l1w5zj68
🤖 Make.com: https://www.make.com/en/register?pc=marcomindstone
🤖 Fathom: https://fathom.video/invite/QcTA1A (1 month of Fathom Premium for FREE!)
📅 Morgen: https://morgen.so/?ref=n2jkyjy
🎓 Tana Course: https://tanacentral.com/marco-tana-one
📄 Typeform: https://typeform.cello.so/4OFb9Vvqyze
⌘ Raycast: https://raycast.com/?via=marco-serafini
📅 Vimcal: https://www.vimcal.com/?via=marco

-- CONTACTS --
- Website: https://mindstoneconsulting.net/
- 𝕏: https://x.com/Marco_Mindstone
- Mastodon: https://mstdn.business/@Marco_Mindstone
- Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/marco-mindstone.bsky.social
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/marco-serafini-529468217/
- Medium: https://medium.com/@Marco_Mindstone
- Discord Server: https://discord.gg/Kn97v27D7p

-- TIMESTAMPS --
- 00:00 Intro
- 00:30 The Process
- 02:53 Outro & CTA

-- MUSIC --
Song: Memories
Artist: Owl Nest
Music by: https://CreatorMix.com
Video: https://youtu.be/mBVBmnNM-Cc
```

## Transcript

Hey everyone, welcome back to the channel! We all know how crucial it is to have a
solid system for managing meetings. In previous videos, we explored how
Fathom can help streamline workflows and ensure you never miss a single detail.
But what if you’re someone who likes to back up your meeting video recordings? If that sounds
like you, you’re in the right place! In today’s video, I’ll show you how
to automatically download and save your meeting recordings from Fathom directly
into Google Drive using Zapier. So, let’s dive in and get started!
So, when you record a meeting with Fathom, it’s automatically saved to your account, complete
with detailed notes, transcript, highlights, and action items. From there, you can easily
share the meeting or download the video recording. Everything you need is right at your fingertips,
all in one place! But here’s the kicker: why stop there when you
can save even more time with automations? Let’s jump on Zapier and create a new Zap.
For the trigger, select Fathom and choose “New Download Video” as the trigger event.
Next, connect your Fathom account and click “Continue”. Select the meeting group you want
to track—in my case, it’s “My Meetings”. Once selected, click “Continue” and test the trigger.
Zapier will either create a sample meeting or retrieve one from your existing recorded meetings.
When that’s done, click “Continue with selected record” to move on and connect Google Drive.
For the action event, select “Upload File”. Connect your Google account, and click “Continue.”
Here’s where you set up the file upload. First, choose the "Drive" and "Folder" where
you want to store your video recordings. In the "File" field, specify the file
to upload. For this, I use the “Download Video Download URL” retrieved from Fathom.
Set “Convert to document?” to false, and if you want, you can specify a custom
file name. If you leave this blank, the meeting’s name will be used automatically.
Once everything is set, click “Continue” and then “Test Step”.
If all goes well, you’ll see the video recording appear in the Google Drive
folder you selected. Open the folder, and voilà, your meeting recording is right there!
And that’s it! Now, to replicate this automation, you’ll need at least the Fathom Premium plan,
which unlocks the Zapier integration, along with other great AI features. If
you’d like to try it out, you can do so for free using my referral link, which
gives you a full month of Premium at no cost. You’ll find the link in the description below.
Having a reliable system to keep track of your meetings is more important than ever,
and Fathom has you covered! Remember, you can follow and
connect with me on X, Mastodon, and Bluesky. And you can also join my Discord server and
subscribe to my newsletter for more content about productivity, knowledge management and automation.
All the links are in the description below! Thank you so much for watching,
and I'll see you in the next video. In the meantime...stay productive!

Last update 4 months ago

Categories

Share

[Share on X](https://x.com/intent/post?url=https%3A%2F%2Fn8n.io%2Fworkflows%2F9549-convert-fathom-meeting-transcripts-to-formatted-google-docs-with-ai-summaries%2F)[Share on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fn8n.io%2Fworkflows%2F9549-convert-fathom-meeting-transcripts-to-formatted-google-docs-with-ai-summaries%2F)

* * *

_Transcript → AI Analysis → Formatted Doc_

This workflow automatically converts Fathom meeting transcripts into beautifully formatted Google Docs with AI-generated summaries, key points, decisions, and action items.

### Good to know

- Works fully with Fathom free account
- Webhook responds immediately to prevent Fathom timeout and duplicate triggers
- Validates transcript quality (3+ conversation turns) before AI processing to save costs
- Uses Google Gemini API (generous free tier and rate limits, typically enough to avoid paying for API requests, but check latest pricing at [Google AI Pricing](https://ai.google.dev/pricing))
- Creates temporary HTML file that's auto-deleted after conversion

## Who's it for

Individuals or teams using Fathom for meetings who want more control and flexibility with their AI meeting analysis and storage independently of Fathom, as well as automatic, formatted documentation without manual note-taking. Perfect for recurring syncs, client meetings, or interview debriefs.

## How it works

1. Fathom webhook triggers when meeting ends and sends transcript data
2. Validates transcript has meaningful conversation (3+ turns)
3. Google Gemini AI analyzes transcript and generates structured summary (key points, decisions, actions, next steps)
4. Creates formatted HTML with proper styling
5. Uploads to Google Drive and converts to native Google Doc
6. Reduces page margins for readability and deletes temporary HTML file

## Requirements

- Fathom account with API webhook access (available on free tier)
- Google Drive account (OAuth2)
- Google Docs account (OAuth2)
- Google Gemini API key ( [Get free key here](https://makersuite.google.com/app/apikey))

## How to set up

1. Add credentials: Google Drive OAuth2, Google Docs OAuth2, Google Gemini API
2. Copy the webhook URL from the Get Fathom Meeting webhook node (Test URL first, change to Production URL when ready)
3. In Fathom: Settings → API Access → Add → Add webhook URL and select all events including "Transcript"
4. Test with a short meeting, verify Google Doc appears in Drive
5. Activate workflow

## Customizing this workflow

- **Change save location**: Edit "Upload File as HTML" node → update "Parent Folder"
- **Modify AI output**: Edit "AI Meeting Analysis" node → customize the prompt to add/remove sections (e.g., risks, follow-ups, sentiment, etc)
- **Adjust document margins**: Edit "Reduce Page Margins" node → change margin pixel values
- **Add notifications**: E.g. add Slack/Email node after "Convert to Google Doc" to notify team when summary is ready

## **Quick Troubleshooting**

- **"Transcript Present?" fails**: Fathom must send `transcript_merged` with 3+ conversation turns (i.e. only send to Gemini for analysis if there's a genuine transcript)
- **HTML as plain text**: Check "Convert to Google Doc" uses POST to `/copy` endpoint
- **401/403 errors**: Re-authorize Google credentials
- **Inadequate meeting notes**: Edit prompts in "AI Meeting Analysis" node

## Sample File and Storage Output

- [Google Doc meeting notes - sample](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1tCC90dIpgb8NtuOJ_jSTCPn4MxORB-XcvwdeljYzC9w/edit?usp=drive_link)
- Google Drive sample folder output:

## Overview

Records, transcribes & summarizes meetings FREE

Install this Fathom for Google Meet Chrome extension to unlock:

\\* Ability to use Fathom on any Google Meet - without it you have to make sure to join the meeting from the Fathom desktop app
\\* Embedded recording controls right inside Google Meet, so you never have to dig for the desktop app
\\* Instant join for any Meet link you click — even unscheduled calls

ABOUT FATHOM

It’s AI notetaking for the way you work. Fathom captures, transcribes, and summarizes your Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams meetings, now available bot-free, so you can stay fully present in the conversation and let the notes take themselves.

🆓 Free forever. Unlimited recording, transcription, and storage. Get set up in minutes.
🥇 #1 rated AI Notetaker on G2, used at 300K+ companies.
🔒 Enterprise-grade security: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, and HIPAA compliant, with SSO/SCIM available.

KEY FEATURES

⚡ Instant, Shockingly Accurate Summaries: The moment your call ends, you get a clean transcript, a high-quality summary, and AI-generated action items delivered straight to your inbox. Turn a 30-minute call into a 1-minute read.

🪐 Ask Fathom – Across Every Meeting You've Ever Had: A ChatGPT-style interface for your entire meeting history. Ask things like "What did the customer say about pricing last quarter?" or "Summarize every conversation about the Acme deal" and get instant, cited answers.

🛸 Bot-Free Capture: Record meetings without a bot joining as a visible participant. Stay present, stay private, and skip the "who invited the notetaker?" conversation.

🤖 ChatGPT & Claude Integrations: Bring your meeting intelligence into the AI tools you already use. Connect Fathom to ChatGPT or Claude and pull insights, draft follow-ups, or analyze patterns across calls.

🎯 AI Scorecards & Coaching: Auto-grade calls against custom criteria — perfect for sales, customer success, and interview workflows. Managers coach with confidence; reps level up faster.

🔥 Auto-Updates Salesforce, HubSpot, and Asana: No more post-call data entry. Fathom logs the call, writes the summary, updates the right fields, and creates tasks in your project tools — automatically.

🔔 Keyword & Topic Alerts: Get notified the moment a competitor, feature request, or critical topic gets mentioned in any meeting across your team.

🙏 Easily Share Highlights & Clips: Show, don't tell. Clip key moments from any call and share them with teammates, prospects, or leadership in a couple of clicks.

🔌 Works with the tools you already use: Slack, Notion, Gmail, Zapier, plus a Public API and MCP server for custom workflows.

See more

# 10 Fathom AI Alternatives & Competitors \[2026\]

![Sanduni](https://www.meetjamie.ai/api/media/file/sanduni%20linkedin.png?prefix=payload)

## By Sanduni

## Growth Content Editor

![10 Fathom AI Alternatives & Competitors [Updated September 2025]](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2F10_Fathom_AI_Alternatives_Competitors_Updated_September_2025-hk4q67.png%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-09-26T08%253A00%253A51.647Z&w=3840&q=75)

**Fathom AI's** flashy pop-ups, bright yellow speaker time tracker, and the bot inside the meeting were too overwhelming for me, so I searched for **Fathom AI Alternatives.**

First of all, I like to ramble, go off on tangents, and make jokes. I also like to have real, human conversations. Fathom AI's bot, however, **lurking** on the screen trying to summarize what's being said makes it a _**little awkward.**_

It was not only me who got a little _**nervous**_ about being recorded; other people also found it hard to be themselves with a robot on the screen. This made me quickly look into Best Fathom AI alternatives _**(that especially take amazing notes without BOTS).**_

In this post, I’ll walk you through why someone would want to look into **Fathom AI alternatives,** and then I’ll share a list of the **Top 10 Fathom AI competitors** to upgrade your meeting game. You’ll find options that prioritize your privacy, AI-powered show-stoppers, and everything in between.

Let’s get to it!

## Capture your first meeting

Jamie transcribes your computer's audio directly, with no meeting bots joining your call. Available for MacOS and Windows.

[Learn more](https://docs.meetjamie.ai/pages/getting_started/quickstart) [Start Jamie](https://app.meetjamie.ai/)

‍

## **TL;DR**

- Fathom AI is frustrating people because its visible bot, distracting pop ups, and inaccurate summaries interrupt natural conversations and make meetings feel less human.
- People want a tool that gives accurate transcripts, clear summaries with action items, identifies speakers, respects privacy, works offline or in person, runs fast, is searchable, fits into calendars and workflows, and stays compliant.
- Jamie supports these needs by offering instant transcription, accurate summaries with tasks and decisions, intelligent speaker identification, over 100 language options, calendar sync, tagging, Ask AI search, offline and in person recording, editing and collaboration features, seamless copy paste to apps, fast processing, and strict GDPR compliant privacy with audio deletion and encryption.
- Other options include Otter.ai, Fireflies.ai, MeetGeek, and Tactiq for real time transcription and productivity, Avoma and Gong.io for sales and revenue insights, Rewatch and Grain for video management and sharing, and Sembly AI for structured notes and task automation.

‍

## Why You Might Look for Fathom AI Alternatives

### It’s Hard to Ignore That Bot

Fathom’s bot joining as a participant in your own Zoom calls or Teams meetings can feel disruptive and overly present. Many users have expressed discomfort with its visible presence, which they say distracts from the natural flow of conversations.

“I don’t like the presence of the bot as a video attendee on the main screen. It’s distracting, particularly in one-on-one meetings because I’d rather be looking at people on the screen.” **\- G2 Review**

“Hard to make it leave the meeting, when you don’t intend to, also — it being explicitly present as a participant in Zoom.” **\- G2 Review**

### Those Distracting Bars and Pop-Ups

Fathom’s interface isn’t without its flaws. Several users have pointed out that the bars and pop-ups on the screen can feel obtrusive, making it hard to focus on the actual meeting.

“Bars on the right of the screen are distracting.” **\- G2 Review**

### Summaries Aren’t Always Spot On

While Fathom’s note-taking can be helpful to record meetings in, some users have reported that the generated summaries are not always accurate or complete. This can lead to frustration when relying on the tool for detailed meeting documentation.

“Quite a lot of mistakes in the meeting summary, more than acceptable.” **\- G2 Review**

“It’s a little slow in generating alternative summaries. I mostly copy and paste the transcript into Aurora or [ChatGPT](https://www.meetjamie.ai/blog/chat-gpt-4) and get a faster reply.” **\- G2 Review**

[Source](https://www.g2.com/products/fathom-2022-10-26/reviews)

‍

## **What Are the Best Alternatives to Fathom AI?**

The best Fathom AI alternatives are Jamie with its bot-free privacy-first note taking and Ask AI search with CRM integrations, Otter.ai, and Fireflies.ai.

Here’s a breakdown of the 10 Fathom AI competitor tools that I researched:

💜 Gentle Reminder: Pricing may change; please double-check on each tool’s official site. Plans evolve, and enterprise tiers often require a quick chat with sales for accurate quotes.

‍

## \#1: Jamie

Jamie is a **bot-free** [**AI meeting assistant**](https://www.meetjamie.ai/blog/ai-meeting-assistant) and is all about securing **genuine human connections.** Privacy and simplicity are top priorities, and Jamie fits right into your existing video conferencing workflows. Jamie's bot-free feature is helpful when you’re on a sensitive client call and you want to take notes and capture [action items](https://www.meetjamie.ai/blog/action-items).

Jamie _ONLY_ transcribes, meaning it does not record audio (it's deleted right after transcription) or video to maintain your utmost privacy. You can transcribe in whatever video call app you use and Jamie simply gets to work. No bots intruding on your meetings sales calls; _**just amazing notes.**_

‍

## Key Features of Jamie

### Get readable summaries and clear action items instead of messy transcripts

![Jamie extracts highly accurate action items from your audio, whether the meeting takes place online or offline .png](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2FJamie%27s%2520Action%2520items%2520feature%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-11-06T19%253A34%253A10.594Z&w=3840&q=75)

You stop digging through walls of text to find what was decided. Jamie structures every meeting into readable summaries organized by topic, pulls out specific action items with the right people assigned, and gives you clean transcripts you can edit in seconds.

- **Meeting notes**: Get topic-organized summaries with speaker quotes that help you remember the exact conversation
- **Action items**: See captured tasks with assigned owners automatically pulled from the discussion
- **Transcription**: Access instant transcripts for every meeting that are readable and searchable
- **Editing**: Make changes to summaries or transcripts with formatting tools, find-replace, and link insertion
- **Tagging**: Organize meetings with tags so you can filter and find them later

### Remember who said what without replaying the recording

![Jamie’s Dashboard With Meeting Notes, Action Items and Speakers](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2FJamie%25E2%2580%2599s%2520Dashboard%2520With%2520Meeting%2520Notes%252C%2520Action%2520Items%2520and%2520Speakers-1.Jamie%25E2%2580%2599s%2520Dashboard%2520With%2520Meeting%2520Notes%252C%2520Action%2520Items%2520and%2520Speakers%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262026-04-22T12%253A43%253A36.443Z&w=3840&q=75)

You skip the confusion of "who was that again" in your notes. Jamie identifies speakers, remembers voices across meetings, syncs with your calendar to auto-populate attendee names, generates descriptive meeting titles automatically, and sends optional consent emails to participants before recording.

- **Speaker identification**: Jamie labels speakers in transcripts so you know who contributed each point
- **Speaker memory**: The tool remembers voices from past meetings and links names automatically for future calls
- **Calendar integration**: Connect Google or Outlook calendar to sync meeting details and populate attendee information
- **Automatic meeting titles**: Get meaningful titles generated for ad-hoc meetings instead of generic time stamps
- **Consent emails**: Send automatic notifications to attendees 24 hours before meetings informing them about recording

### Work anywhere without platform restrictions or privacy concerns

![jamie is a bot free ai note taker,  you just have to press start jamie and it will capture your online or offline meeting's audio and transcribe it to intelligent transcriptions and meeting notes](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2FJamie%27s%2520offline%2520and%2520online%2520feature%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-11-06T19%253A01%253A45.960Z&w=3840&q=75)

You avoid the awkwardness of bots joining calls and platform compatibility headaches. Jamie works as a native application on your device, captures meetings online or offline, supports in-person discussions, handles conversations in any language, and keeps everything secure with European privacy standards.

- **Bot-free operation**: No virtual bot joins your calls, Jamie captures system audio directly from your device
- **Cross-platform support**: Works on macOS, Windows, and iOS so you can record meetings on any device
- **Online and offline meetings**: Capture notes during video calls, in-person meetings, or offline discussions without internet
- **100+ languages**: Transcribe and generate summaries in over 100 languages with automatic language detection
- **GDPR-compliant encryption**: All data encrypted with AES in transit and at rest, stored in Frankfurt Germany servers
- **iOS mobile app**: Record in-person meetings from your phone with same features as desktop

### Push meeting insights into your tools without manual data entry

![Your meeting notes, tasks, and transcripts flow straight into HubSpot, Attio, Asana, and Notion, so no one has to copy and paste again.](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2FJamie%27s%2520Integration%2520Feature%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-11-05T07%253A47%253A37.265Z&w=3840&q=75)

You stop copying and pasting meeting notes into five different systems. Jamie connects directly to your CRM and productivity apps, sends summaries and tasks automatically, and preserves formatting when you need to move content manually.

- **Salesforce integration**: Send summaries and transcripts as notes to any Salesforce record like leads or opportunities
- **HubSpot integration**: Push meeting notes to HubSpot contacts, deals, or companies automatically or manually
- **Attio integration**: Sync summaries and transcripts to Attio person or company records as notes
- **Asana integration**: Send action items as tasks to Asana projects with summary attached as PDF
- **Notion and OneNote**: Automatically or manually sync meeting notes to Notion databases or OneNote pages
- **Webhooks**: Set up custom integrations through Zapier or Make using webhook triggers for new meetings
- **Copy-paste compatibility**: Preserve formatting and task status when pasting into tools like Linear, Todoist, or Bear

### Get answers and insights without reading every transcript

![You can chat with your notes with Jamie's Ask AI and instantly get the exact quote, action item, translation, or just ask questions from any client meetings. ](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2FJamie%27s%2520Ask%2520AI%2520Feature%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-11-05T07%253A49%253A53.826Z&w=3840&q=75)

You avoid scrolling through hours of past meetings hunting for one detail. Jamie lets you ask questions about your meetings, jot quick thoughts during calls, structure notes with custom templates, and search across all your meeting history.

- **Ask AI**: Ask questions about single meetings or search across all your meetings to find specific information
- **Scratch Pad**: Jot down quick thoughts, follow-ups, or reminders during meetings without switching tabs or apps
- **Templates**: Apply predefined note structures for different meeting types like sales calls, standups, or interviews
- **Search**: Find past meetings and specific topics across your entire meeting history with searchable transcripts

‍

### Pricing

![Jamie Pricing Plan | Fathom AI Alternatives](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2FJamie_Pricing_Plan_Fathom_AI_Alternatives-5jatq7.png%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-09-26T08%253A00%253A25.779Z&w=3840&q=75)

- **Free:** €0/month
- **Standard:** €24/month
- **Pro:** €47/month
- **Executive:** €99/month

‍

### Fathom AI Vs Jamie

Jamie and Fathom AI both offer AI meeting tools, but they work very differently. Jamie is completely bot-free. It values your privacy and takes notes without disrupting your meetings. It doesn't join your calls or show up on screen. Jamie only transcribes audio, never video. It deletes all recordings after processing them. It works offline and for in-person meetings too. This makes Jamie perfect for sensitive meetings or casual conversations where you need things to flow naturally. Fathom AI puts a visible bot in your calls. Many users find this distracting during important meetings. Fathom is good at live transcription and connects with CRM systems just like Jamie, which helps sales teams track conversations.

For better view of both compare, look at the comparison table below;

When it comes to meeting automation tools, both Jamie and Fathom have their own strengths. Let me break it down for you so you can decide which one is for you.

#### Where Fathom excels

Fathom is good if your workflow is built around CRM tools like Salesforce or HubSpot. It can sync meeting notes directly into these systems so is perfect for sales and customer success teams who need integration.

If you need real-time transcription, Fathom might be for you. It offers live transcription with speaker attribution so you can see what’s being discussed and who said what.

#### Where Jamie excels

Jamie takes a different approach, prioritizing privacy, flexibility, and an intuitive user experience. Here’s why I think Jamie has the edge in a few areas:

- **Bot-Free Experience:** Jamie doesn’t join your meetings as a visible participant. This means no interruptions or awkward bots on the screen. You can focus on the conversation, not the tool.
- **Privacy You Can Trust:** If privacy is important to you, Jamie wins. Unlike Fathom, Jamie transcribes audio, deletes it immediately after processing, and doesn’t record video. This makes it perfect for sensitive discussions like HR meetings, strategy sessions, or legal matters.
- **Offline and In-Person Capabilities:** Jamie isn’t just for virtual meetings. It can transcribe in-person discussions, even without internet access. Whether you’re at an offsite, a workshop or an in-office brainstorming session, Jamie means you never miss a word.
- **Sidebar feature**: Jamie acts as an AI-powered executive assistant. With a simple shortcut (CTRL + J), you can search past meetings, ask questions, or even draft emails, saving time and increasing productivity.
- **Universal compatibility**: Jamie works across all video conferencing platforms without requiring plugins or extensions, ensuring a seamless experience regardless of the platform you or your clients use.

## Capture your first meeting

Jamie transcribes your computer's audio directly, with no meeting bots joining your call. Available for MacOS and Windows.

[Learn more](https://docs.meetjamie.ai/pages/getting_started/quickstart) [Start Jamie](https://app.meetjamie.ai/)

‍

## \#2: Otter.ai

![Otter ai | Fathom AI alternative](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2FOtter_ai_Fathom_AI_alternative-g5875d.png%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-09-26T08%253A00%253A22.218Z&w=3840&q=75)

Otter.ai is an AI meeting assistant with automated, meeting transcripts, notes, summaries and action items via its OtterPilot™ technology. It does real-time transcription and has tools to help you engage and be more productive in meetings.

- **Best for:** Real-time transcription, automated summaries, productivity across meetings.
- **Similar to:** Fireflies.ai, MeetGeek.

### Who Is It For?

Otter.ai is for teams and professionals who want accurate transcription, automated summaries and workflow integration to be more productive.

### Fathom AI Vs Otter AI

Fathom gives you instant meeting summaries, better transcription accuracy, and highlights key points in real-time. Otter shows you live transcripts, lets your team collaborate during meetings, and includes an AI helper (OtterPilot). Otter's free version is pretty limited, while Fathom gives you a more generous free plan.

### Otter.ai’s Top Features

- **Live Transcription:** Real-time transcription during meetings with high accuracy.
- **Automatic Summaries:** Turns long meetings into short summaries.
- **Task Assignment:** Identifies and assigns tasks during meetings.
- **Integrations:** Works with Slack, Salesforce, Google Meet.

### Otter.ai Pros and Cons

**Cons**

[(source)](https://www.g2.com/products/otter-ai/reviews?utf8=%E2%9C%93&order=most_recent)

‍

## \#3: Fireflies.ai

![fireflies | Fathom AI alternatives](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2Ffireflies_Fathom_AI_alternatives-msd4pw.png%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-09-26T08%253A00%253A34.902Z&w=3840&q=75)

- **Best for:** Team collaboration, conversation analysis, automated workflows.
- **Similar to:** Otter.ai, MeetGeek.

### Who Is It For?

Fireflies.ai is for teams that need to transcribe and analyze meetings, collaborate and automate workflows.

### Fathom AI Vs Fireflies

Fathom gives fast, clear summaries and works well with CRMs. Fireflies has real-time transcription, more app connections, and deeper insights. Fathom is quicker and easier; Fireflies has more features.

### Fireflies.ai’s Top Features

- **AI Transcription:** Multi-language meeting transcription.
- **Meeting Summaries:** Automatically summarizes with action items.

‍

## \#4: MeetGeek

![MeetGeek | Fathom AI Alternatives](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2FMeetGeek_Fathom_AI_Alternatives-j0i0nt.png%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-09-26T08%253A00%253A34.828Z&w=3840&q=75)

### MeetGeek’s Top Features

- **AI Meeting Summaries:** Generates meeting summaries with action items and key points.
- **Seamless Integrations:** 7,000+ apps, Slack, HubSpot, Google Drive, Zapier.

‍

## \#5: Avoma

![avoma | Fathom ai alternative](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2Favoma_Fathom_ai_alternative-it23wo.png%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-09-26T08%253A00%253A33.282Z&w=3840&q=75)

- Multi-language transcription and CRM integration.

#### **Cons**

- Speed issues in navigation and loading.

## My Final (Honest) Thoughts on Fathom AI Note Taker

**Fathom** [**AI Note Taker**](https://www.meetjamie.ai/blog/ai-note-taker) is a decent [**automated note taker**](https://www.meetjamie.ai/blog/automated-meeting-notes) but it has its limitations, especially when it comes to a more **personal and natural meeting experience.**

![contrasting images of how jamie shows their timers, and how fathom shows theirs. ](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2Fcontrasting_images_of_how_jamie_shows_their_timers_and_how_fathom_shows_theirs.-fbfjgg.png%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-09-26T08%253A00%253A41.444Z&w=3840&q=75)

During meetings, **Fathom’s bot can feel intrusive**, especially the **bright yellow “Monologue Detection” pop-up,** which interrupts the flow of conversation and makes participants **self-conscious.** This feature is meant to ensure equal speaking time by timing your talk time, but is more of a distraction than a benefit, in my opinion.

![monologue detection](https://www.meetjamie.ai/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.meetjamie.ai%2Fapi%2Fmedia%2Ffile%2Fmonologue_detection-tz508w.png%3Fprefix%3Dpayload%262025-09-26T08%253A00%253A41.022Z&w=640&q=75)

And the **pop-up box that stays on the screen even after attendees have joined** just adds to the frustration.

After the meeting, the **dashboard provided by Fathom was functional but cluttered and hard to navigate.**

The **condensed meeting notes** make it hard to find specific information quickly. Although Fathom offers options to highlight and bookmark during the meeting, the execution isn’t always smooth and sometimes misses the mark.

In contrast, **Jamie is a more streamlined experience** with **simplicity, privacy, and user control.** **Jamie has no bots** and a **non-intrusive interface** so it’s a more natural meeting environment.

**Jamie’s advanced AI models** have **broader and more complex conversational abilities** than Fathom’s basic chat functions. **Jamie also deletes audio after transcription** and doesn’t record video.

If you need detailed automated notes with CRM integration and don’t mind a more intrusive tool, you will love Fathom.

But if you want a more **natural, less distracting meeting experience with more privacy and more powerful AI, Jamie might be the better option.** Not only will you get an exclusive meeting experience, you will also be provided with a **human-like** [**meeting summary**](https://www.meetjamie.ai/blog/write-meeting-summary).

Try both Fathom and Jamie and see which one fits your meeting needs. Fathom has good features but requires some adjustments to your personal taste, while **Jamie has a more hands-off, user-friendly approach** that could **improve your meeting experience.**

Fathom provides high-quality video integration, allowing users to capture, review, and share specific clips from meetings. The video quality is generally reliable, and combined with its accurate transcription capabilities, it makes for a comprehensive meeting recording tool.

### **Does Fathom Notetaker work with Teams?**

Yes, Fathom is fully compatible with Microsoft Teams. It can automatically detect Teams meetings from your calendar, record them, and provide real-time transcription with speaker attribution. After the meeting, the full transcript is available in the meeting chat for all attendees to review and download.

### **Is Fathom AI free?**

Fathom AI offers a free version with unlimited storage and recordings, making it accessible to individual users at no cost. For more advanced features, users can upgrade to paid plans like Fathom Premium or Fathom for Teams.

### **Does Fathom offer a Chrome extension?**

Yes, Fathom offers a Chrome extension that integrates seamlessly with Google Meet. This extension allows users to easily capture and transcribe meetings directly within their browser, adding to Fathom’s versatility across different platforms.

# Convert Fathom meeting transcripts to formatted Google Docs with AI summaries

Automatically transform raw meeting transcripts into professional, searchable documents with key insights highlighted

[Download Template](https://ik.imagekit.io/r2zdlyze2o/GS-Website/N8N-Workflows/9549-convert-fathom-meeting-transcripts-to-formatted-google-.json?ik-attachment=true) JSON · n8n compatible · Free

![Workflow diagram showing Fathom to Google Docs conversion process](https://ik.imagekit.io/r2zdlyze2o/GS-Website/N8N-Workflows/9549-convert-fathom-meeting-transcripts-to-formatted-google-.png)

## What This Workflow Does

This n8n workflow automatically converts raw Fathom meeting transcripts into professionally formatted Google Docs complete with AI-generated summaries. It eliminates hours of manual transcription work while ensuring consistent documentation across all your meetings.

The system captures every spoken word from your Fathom recordings, structures the content with proper headings and formatting, then adds intelligent summaries highlighting key decisions, action items, and discussion points. The result is searchable, shareable meeting documentation that helps teams stay aligned without tedious note-taking.

![Sample output showing formatted Google Doc with meeting transcript and AI summary](https://i.postimg.cc/MH8Vtny4/Sample-Google-Drive-Output.png)

Example of automatically generated Google Doc with formatted transcript and AI summary section

## How It Works

### 1\. Trigger from new Fathom recording

The workflow activates whenever Fathom generates a new meeting transcript. It captures the raw text along with meeting metadata like participants, duration, and timestamps.

### 2\. AI analysis and summarization

An AI processing step identifies key discussion points, decisions made, and action items. It generates concise executive summaries while preserving the full transcript context.

### 3\. Document formatting

The system applies your preferred formatting template—adding headings, speaker labels, timestamps, and visual separation between sections. Formatting rules ensure consistency across all meeting docs.

### 4\. Google Doc creation

The formatted content saves as a new Google Doc in your specified Drive folder. The workflow can optionally notify participants or share the doc automatically.

**Pro tip:** Configure the workflow to add @mentions for action items automatically, making responsibilities clear in the document.

## Who This Is For

This automation is ideal for teams that:

- Conduct frequent client meetings or internal discussions
- Need accurate records of conversations for compliance or reference
- Want to reduce time spent on manual note-taking and transcription
- Share meeting outcomes with stakeholders who weren't present
- Value searchable archives of past discussions

## What You'll Need

1. An active Fathom account with recording permissions
2. n8n instance (cloud or self-hosted)
3. Google Workspace account with Docs API access
4. Access to an AI service like OpenAI for summarization

## Quick Setup Guide

1. Download the workflow template file
2. Import into your n8n instance
3. Connect your Fathom and Google accounts
4. Configure your preferred document formatting

## Frequently Asked Questions

Common questions about meeting transcription and documentation automation

What are the benefits of automatically converting meeting transcripts to Google Docs?↓

Automating meeting transcript conversion saves hours of manual work while improving document consistency. The AI summaries help teams quickly extract key decisions and action items. Formatted documents are easier to reference later and can be automatically shared with stakeholders.

This workflow reduces human error in transcription and ensures all meeting notes follow the same professional format. Sales teams use it to automatically generate follow-up materials, while HR departments benefit from standardized interview documentation.

- Creates searchable knowledge base over time

How accurate are AI-generated meeting summaries?↓

Modern AI summarization tools achieve 85-95% accuracy for meeting transcripts when properly configured. The summaries focus on extracting key points, decisions, and action items rather than verbatim transcription.

Accuracy improves when the AI has context about meeting participants and objectives. Most businesses find the summaries sufficiently accurate for internal use after minor human review. For client-facing documents, many teams add a quick verification step.

- Configure with your industry terminology
- Provide meeting agendas when possible
- Review first few summaries to train the AI

What types of meetings work best with automated transcription?↓

Structured meetings with clear agendas translate best to automated transcripts. Sales calls, team standups, and client consultations work particularly well. Technical discussions with specialized jargon may require more editing.

The system works best for 1:1 or small group meetings under 60 minutes where participants speak clearly without excessive crosstalk. Board meetings and legal discussions often need tighter security configurations.

- Ideal for: Sales demos, interviews, sprint planning
- Good for: Team syncs, client check-ins
- Needs tuning: Technical deep dives, brainstorming

Can I customize the Google Doc formatting?↓

Yes, the workflow template allows full customization of the Google Doc output format. You can modify headings, font styles, color schemes, and section organization.

Common customizations include adding company branding, standardized headers/footers, and tailored section layouts for different meeting types. The formatting rules apply automatically to every converted transcript.

- Create multiple templates for different meetings
- Add your logo and brand colors
- Include standard disclaimer text

How does this compare to manual note-taking?↓

Automated transcription captures 3-4x more content than manual notes with perfect recall of who said what. While humans focus on key points, AI preserves full context.

The hybrid approach of AI transcription plus human review of summaries combines the best of both. Teams report 60-80% time savings versus traditional minute-taking while improving documentation quality.

- Never miss important details again
- Reference exact wording when needed
- Focus on discussion rather than notes

What security measures protect sensitive meeting content?↓

The workflow uses encrypted connections between services and allows restricting Google Doc access. You can configure the system to store transcripts only in specific secured folders.

For highly sensitive meetings, some organizations add a manual approval step before document creation. All AI processing occurs through reputable providers with enterprise-grade data protection.

- Set granular Google Drive permissions
- Enable two-factor authentication
- Audit access logs regularly
